commentaries.author: Hippolytus of Rome
commentaries.content: By whom the world moves; by whom creation consists, and all things have life; who also wrought mightily in the prophets,

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: See again how not by miracles but by Law and Prophets he puts them to silence, and how we always find him doing this. And yet he might also have wrought signs; but then it would no longer have been matter of faith. In fact, this (itself) was a great sign, his discoursing from the Law and the Prophets. Then that you may not deem it strange (that they believed not), he introduces the prophecy which says, Hearing you shall hear and not understand, more now than then: and you shall see and not perceive
